question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $112,000 on October 31?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Binance BTC-USDT Liquidity Falls 70% after Revoking Zero-fee ...
evidences.content: In this article: BTC/USD+3.08% Threshold US Dollar+2.95% Binance BTC liquidity for BUSD and USDT dropped by almost 70%. Conversely, the BTC-TUSD trading pair saw a 250% hike within 24 hours. Traders bought and sold over $18.5 billion of crypto Binance in the past 24 hours. Data from Kaiko, a market intelligence firm, shows that the liquidity for BTC-USDT and BTC-BUSD trading pairs on the most prominent crypto marketplace, Binance, has dropped significantly by almost 70% and 60%, respectively. [...] On the flip side, the BTC-TUSD trading pair has seen an impressive increase of over 250% in liquidity, going from just 9 Bitcoin to 29 BTC within a 24-hour window. A researcher at the data analytic firm noted that the considerable drop in liquidity was due to Binance’s recent decision to remove its flagship no-fee trading feature for all BTC pairs except BTC/TUSD. > Binance BTC-USDT liquidity has dropped by nearly 70% since zero-fee trading was phased out earlier todayBTC-BUSD liquidity has dropped 60% in the same timeframeBTC-TUSD up over 250% (from just 9 BTC to 29) pic.twitter.com/4Qr39KUIQi > — Riyad Carey (@riyad_carey) March 22, 2023 [...] It’s worth noting that the Bitcoin pair...

evidences.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Price Prediction 2026–2030
evidences.content: ### Will Bitcoin Go Up in 2026? Many analysts believe Bitcoin has the potential to recover during 2026, although volatility is likely to remain high. Spot Bitcoin ETFs continue attracting institutional capital, public companies keep adding BTC to their balance sheets, and regulatory clarity in the United States has improved compared to previous years. At the same time, Bitcoin remains well below its October 2025 all-time high, leaving room for recovery if investor confidence strengthens. However, risks such as weaker economic growth, ETF outflows, and higher interest rates could delay the next bull market. Most forecasts remain cautiously optimistic, expecting Bitcoin to finish 2026 above its current price despite continued short-term fluctuations. [...] ### Bitcoin Price Forecast June–December 2026 Forecasts for the second half of 2026 highlight two very different market outlooks. DigitalCoinPrice expects Bitcoin to move within a relatively narrow range, gradually rising from around $59,000 in June to approximately $64,200 by December. This model assumes stable market conditions, moderate institutional demand, and limited volatility. PricePrediction.net presents a much more opt...

evidences.title: $BTC Understanding the BTC/USDT Coin Pair on ...
evidences.content: BTC, as the first and most valuable cryptocurrency, is known for its volatility. Pairing it with USDT helps traders measure Bitcoin’s value in dollar terms and manage risk during market fluctuations. Since USDT maintains a 1:1 peg with the US dollar, it acts as a digital equivalent of cash, allowing traders to lock in profits or avoid losses during market dips without leaving the crypto ecosystem. On Binance, BTC/USDT is among the most liquid pairs, offering tight spreads, deep order books, and fast execution. This makes it ideal for both spot trading and derivatives. The pair is also frequently used in technical analysis and trading bots due to its steady volume and predictable behavior compared to altcoin pairs. [...] Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up DiscoverNewsNotificationProfileBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Post Image 2 rosesareromsee Apr 22, 2025 Follow Report Block User $BTC Understanding the BTC/USDT Coin Pair on Binance The BTC/USDT trading pair is one of the most important and widely used pairs in the world of cryptocurrency. evidences.title: Cryptocurrency Trading Fees | Low-Cost Crypto Exchange | Binance.US | Binance.US Platform
evidences.content: # Binance.US Cryptocurrency Trading Fees Enjoy 0% maker fees on Tier 0 pairs, with no subscription fees or trading volume requirements. Binance.US is the only major U.S. exchange to offer 0% maker fees and 0.01% taker fees for all new and existing customers, with no subscription fees or trading volume requirements. Trading fees are determined by your 30-day trading volume\, calculated on a rolling basis every day at 8 p.m. EDT. \Excludes self-trading and Tier 0 trading pairs Get a 5% discount on Advanced Trading (Spot Trading) fees when you pay with BNB. Check the table below to see how fees vary by tier. [...] Fees are calculated and displayed on the “Preview Purchase” screen for your review before you approve the transaction. Fee calculations differ based on factors such as the digital asset itself, transaction type, payment method, and market conditions such as liquidity and volatility. To lock in prices during the “Preview Purchase” screen, Binance.US includes a spread when you buy, sell or convert crypto. No spread applies to Advanced Trading as you interact directly with our open order book. Crypto deposits are free, though fees may apply when you withdraw crypto. Fees may...

evidences.content: ## Future Outlook ### Predictions for 2025 and Beyond The future outlook for Bitcoin and the cryptocurrency market remains optimistic, with several analysts predicting significant price appreciation. According to a report by Fool.com, the price of Bitcoin could reach nearly $150,000 by 2025 in a base case scenario, driven by continued institutional investment and the approval of Bitcoin ETFs. In a bull case scenario, the price could potentially reach $1 million or more. Bernstein’s report predicts that Bitcoin will reach $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033, driven by further institutional adoption and ETF inflows. ### Factors Influencing Future Trends Several factors will influence future trends in the cryptocurrency market: [...] ## Conclusion The cryptocurrency market is at a pivotal moment, with Bitcoin price predictions and institutional adoption shaping its future trajectory. evidences.title: Bitcoin USD Price: Quote, Market Cap, Forecast & News (BTCUSD)
evidences.content: Bitcoin is trading little changed near $60,000, hovering just above its 52-week low as the market grapples with a confluence of persistent headwinds. Spot Bitcoin ETFs have logged seven consecutive days of outflows totaling billions in withdrawals, while a hotter-than-expected U.S. Core PCE print of 4.1% — a three-year high — has pushed Fed rate cut expectations into late 2026 or 2027, keeping risk appetite suppressed. Strategy (MSTR) is under intensifying pressure with Bitcoin trading well below its ~$75,000 average cost basis and its preferred stock losing its $100 peg. Technically, Bitcoin is consolidating near its 200-week moving average — historically a key accumulation zone — with analysts watching the $58,000 level as critical support, while the CLARITY Act's uncertain legislative [...] Bitcoin is trading nearly 2% higher, rebounding toward $62,600 as May CPI data came in largely in line with expectations — headline inflation rising 4.2% year-over-year with core CPI below forecast at 2.9% — offering modest relief to risk assets after weeks of macro-driven selling pressure. The in-line reading tempered fears of an imminent Fed rate hike ahead of the June 17 meeting, though...

evidences.content: ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:45 Petr Hejda's user avatar Petr Hejda [...] ``` #!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done ``` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: | Open time...

evidences.title: Bitcoin price on October 19? - Polymarket
evidences.content: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar. If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket. Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets. evidences.title: Cryptocurrency Market Dynamics Around Bitcoin Futures Expiration ...
evidences.content: And here comes equity curves (y-axis to depict appreciation/depreciation of $ 1 invested in strategy, or /100 return in % in time): With the traditional performance table: So, if you plan to trade around the BTC futures expiration, you should consider the days around the expiration (Thursday and the weekend afterward + Monday) and avoid the expiration day itself. This pattern is similar to the Option Expiration Week strategy in stock indexes. # Conclusion & Ending [...] An astute reader would see that “price action” (returns around in the vicinity of expiration day) kind of flipped around the dates before and after the BITO launch. The daily pattern in the BTC returns in the period before the BITO introduction (2017-2021) is similar to the whole period (2017-2024). On the other hand, the pattern in the daily returns after the BITO ETF introduction (2021-2024) is reversed, especially on days preceding the expiration and on the expiration day itself. Finally, let’s just quickly look at the returns of BITO ETF (obviously, we track it from its first expiration on futures, which was 10/29/2021, as mentioned earlier): [...] Cboe Bitcoin futures, the first U.S. regulated futures of the...

evidences.title: Open Interest - CME Group
evidences.content: # Open Interest accountID 49919183001 videoID 5625812493001 playlistID playlistLocation R aspectRatio 16:9 cc language placeholderImg Image 1: null ## Understanding Open Interest Open interest is the total number of futures contracts held by market participants at the end of the trading day. It is used as an indicator to determine market sentiment and the strength behind price trends. Unlike the total issued shares of a company, which typically remain constant, the number of outstanding futures contracts varies from day to day. Open interest is calculated by adding all the contracts from opened trades and subtracting the contracts when a trade is closed. [...] For example, Sharon, Cynthia and Kurt are trading the same futures contract. If Sharon buys one contract to enter a long trade, open interest increases by one. Cynthia also goes long and buys six contracts, thereby increasing open interest to seven. If Kurt decides to short the market and sells three contracts, open interest again increases to 10. Open interest would remain at 10 until the traders exit their positions, at which point open interest declines. For example, open interest declines to nine when Sharon sells one...
